codeReview.summary: The feature correctly moved the parser off delimiter-only classification for records beginning at byte 0 and added explicit semantic decode failures, but it still drops leading bytes before the first semantic prefix and still fails to preserve handle/reference-based layer provenance on outward semantic links. Those gaps violate the feature contract because semantic-path decoding is not content-faithful for padded payloads and same-size/content-derived identity is still incomplete for layer references.

issues.description: decode_semantic_section_records slices the payload from the first semantic prefix, silently discarding any leading bytes once contains_semantic_record_prefix() matches anywhere in the payload. For structurally valid padded/prefixed payloads this changes the decoded record bytes and payload_size instead of preserving the actual semantic record boundary/content. The added regression only asserts the truncated sizes 27/31 for the synthetic prefixed fixture, so the feature still does not implement content-faithful semantic boundary decoding required by the contract.

Handle-based layer provenance is only applied to PendingEntity.layer_name via layer_by_handle, but semantic_link() recomputes the outward link using semantic_layer_name_from_fields(), which only succeeds when the same record also contains both LAYER and matching H fields. A record like ENT:LINE:EA1:OA0:LR99 therefore yields layer_handle:99|owner:A0 without the resolved layer name, so outward parser summaries still fail to preserve full content-derived layer provenance for handle/reference-based relationships.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the dwg-parser-worker procedure and produced an isolated feature commit plus focused cargo verification; I did not find a skill/procedure mismatch for this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/dwg-parser-worker/SKILL.md steps 2-9 align with the transcript skeleton and handoff for session 331ded90-fe54-483a-b727-7979a1a5cf7a.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, commit e0287b9b7b13c6ddc81688f896bca2d2e1327f11, and mission/shared-state docs. I found two blocking correctness issues: semantic decoding still trims prefixed bytes before the first semantic marker, and outward semantic summaries still lose resolved layer names for handle-based layer references.
